prompt:
You are the COORDINATOR. A substrate module has PASSED review — it is correct, sorry-free, and reusable. Your job is to INTEGRATE it into the Causalean library cleanly and permanently: put it in the right place, reuse what already exists instead of reinventing, and document it to the project's standard. You do NOT re-judge the math.

## What was built
Staging files (namespace `CausalSmith.Substrate.AffinePolynomialImageDimension.*`) under: /<repo-root>/CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Substrate/AffinePolynomialImageDimension
- /<repo-root>/CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Substrate/AffinePolynomialImageDimension/AffineSpaceDimension.lean
- /<repo-root>/CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Substrate/AffinePolynomialImageDimension/AffineSubspaceDimension.lean

- /<repo-root>/CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Substrate/AffinePolynomialImageDimension/ZariskiClosure.lean

## Requirement it fulfilled
# Substrate requirement: affine-polynomial-image-dimension

## Goal
Build a reusable characteristic-zero bridge from explicit polynomial-coordinate
rank/transcendence certificates to the exact affine Zariski dimension of the
closure of a polynomial image, in the finite-dimensional complex affine setting.
The study imports and builds on neutral modules under
`CausalSmith.Substrate.AffinePolynomialImageDimension`; it must not import the
LiNGAM paper namespace or its `Helpers/` path.

## Provides (API contract)
- `affineZariskiDimension_iff_coordinateRingKrullDim`: for finite `ι` and a
  nonempty affine-closed `Z : Set (ι → ℂ)`, identify the existing chain predicate
  `HasAffineZariskiDimension d Z` with Krull dimension `d` of
  `MvPolynomial ι ℂ ⧸ vanishingIdeal ℂ Z` (or an equivalent canonical statement
  that specializes without new run-specific assumptions).
- `polynomialImageClosure_coordinateRing`: identify the coordinate ring of the
  Zariski closure of the image of a finite polynomial map with the finitely
  generated coordinate subalgebra / `eval₂` image of its coordinate family.
- `polynomialImageClosure_dimension_of_trdeg`: compute exact affine Zariski
  dimension from an exact transcendence-degree certificate for that subalgebra.
- `polynomialImageClosure_dimension_of_jacobian`: a characteristic-zero lower
  bound from a nonzero Jacobian minor, combined with a supplied algebraic or
  fiber-kernel upper certificate to yield exact image-closure dimension.

## Statement / milestones
For finite coordinate types `ι` and `κ`, a polynomial family
`f : κ → MvPolynomial ι ℂ` defines `F : (ι → ℂ) → (κ → ℂ)`.  Its image closure
must have exact `HasAffineZariskiDimension d` whenever the coordinate subalgebra
generated by `f` has transcendence degree `d`.  Supply a usable route for proving
that certificate from an explicit nonzero `d × d` Jacobian minor in
characteristic zero and a matching upper bound (algebraic dependence, kernel, or
fiber-dimension certificate).  All statements must use finite ambients and must
connect to the repository's existing `HasAffineZariskiDimension` definition;
introducing an unrelated dimension notion without proving the bridge is not a
delivery.

## Standard reference
Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, Chapter I (affine varieties, coordinate rings,
dimension); Shafarevich, *Basic Algebraic Geometry 1*, affine morphisms and image
dimension; Matsumura, *Commutative Ring Theory*, Noether normalization and
dimension equals transcendence degree for finite-type domains; the
Jacobian criterion for algebraic independence in characteristic zero.

## Intended reuse
Immediate consumer:
`EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1_Research.exceptionalLocusCodimensionOne`, where
explicit confluent-Vandermonde Jacobian minors give ranks `D` and `D-1` and
compiled low-order weight kernels give the matching upper counts.  The bridge is
general Mathlib-adjacent substrate expected to recur for polynomial
parameterizations, algebraic statistical models, secant/incidence varieties, and
generic-identifiability dimension calculations.

## May assume / must derive
May assume existing Mathlib commutative-algebra theorems with their real names and
hypotheses.  May accept an explicit exact transcendence-degree or upper-dimension
certificate as an input to a high-level specialization lemma.  Must derive the
coordinate-ring/image-closure identification and the bridge to
`HasAffineZariskiDimension`; must not assume the desired image dimension, hide it
in a bespoke structure, or add a LiNGAM-specific premise.  Any classical theorem
absent from Mathlib must be built as the smallest general reusable lemma, with no
`sorry`, custom axioms, or unregistered gates.

## Non-goals (optional)
No LiNGAM cumulant formulas, band-support bookkeeping, confluent-Vandermonde
determinant computation, or model-specific fiber-kernel proof.  No arbitrary
schemes or infinite coordinate types beyond what the finite affine consumer
requires.  Do not replace exact dimension with only irreducibility, nonemptiness,
or a one-sided bound.

## Known building blocks (optional)
Neutral substrate modules define `HasAffineZariskiDimension`, affine-closed
chains, vanishing ideals, polynomial maps, polynomial retracts, and dense image
closures. The paper helper paths remain only as compatibility imports for the
existing LiNGAM development; they are not study dependencies. Inspect
`Helpers/ArrowPolynomialGeometry.lean` only to understand the consumer contract.
Search Mathlib for `ringKrullDim`, `Algebra.TranscendenceDegree`, finite-type
algebras, Noether normalization, `MvPolynomial.aeval`, kernels/ranges, and
Jacobian/algebraic-independence results before creating replacements.

### Existing local substrate reuse map (mandatory)

The current research run originally built substantial general algebraic-
geometry helpers under
`CausalSmith/ExactID/EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1_Research/Helpers/`. They have
now been extracted, without changing their mathematical definitions, into the
neutral `CausalSmith.Substrate.AffinePolynomialImageDimension` module DAG:

- `ZariskiClosure.lean` and `Irreducibility.lean` define
  `affineZariskiClosure` and
  `IsIrreducibleAffineClosed`, proves closure laws,
  `irreducibleAffineClosed_iff_isPrime`, and the strict ideal/zero-locus
  antitonicity lemmas.
- `ChainDimension.lean` defines the exact chain predicate
  `HasAffineZariskiDimension`; `AffineSpaceDimension.lean` proves
  `affineSpace_hasAffineZariskiDimension` using `ringKrullDim`.
- `PolynomialMap.lean` defines `IsPolynomialMap` and proves polynomial
  substitution and preimage/image closure laws;
  `PolynomialRetractDimension.lean` proves retract irreducibility and
  `polynomialRetract_range_dimension`.
- `AffineSubspaceDimension.lean` handles polynomial affine-linear maps and exact
  affine-subspace dimension.
- `DenseImage.lean` proves dense polynomial-image closure and irreducibility
  lemmas. The general opening of `CommonAxisImageGeometry.lean` reexports these
  results, while its later declarations remain LiNGAM-specific consumers.
- `ExceptionalHeightReduction.lean` already converts the two exact dimensions
  `D-1` and `D` into the required height-one/no-intermediate certificate.

Do not create a parallel closure, irreducibility, or dimension language.  Reuse
the neutral declarations directly. The original four paper helper files are
thin compatibility reexports, so existing paper imports and qualified names
continue to work but new substrate code must not depend on them. The genuinely
missing layer is the coordinate-ring/transcendence/Jacobian theorem for general
polynomial-image closures and its compatibility theorem with the existing chain
predicate.

Final promotion to `Causalean` must copy or move only neutral substrate modules
and proofs. No promoted `Causalean` module may import or mention
`CausalSmith.ExactID.EID_LingamDirectionMinOrderV1`, its research module path, or
any paper compatibility wrapper.

### Neutral module DAG and compatibility boundary

The neutral import DAG is:

```text
ZariskiClosure
├── Irreducibility ── ChainDimension ── AffineSpaceDimension
├── PolynomialMap
└── Definitions

AffineSpaceDimension + PolynomialMap
└── PolynomialRetractDimension ── AffineSubspaceDimension

Irreducibility + PolynomialMap
└── DenseImage

Definitions + AffineSpaceDimension
└── CoordinateRing ── Transcendence ── Jacobian

Jacobian + AffineSubspaceDimension + DenseImage
└── CausalSmith.Substrate.AffinePolynomialImageDimension
```

The four paper files `AlgebraicSetChains.lean`, `AffineSpaceDimension.lean`,
`PolynomialRetractDimension.lean`, and `AffineSubspaceDimension.lean` are
compatibility imports/reexports only. They provide the historical paper-qualified
and unqualified API to existing consumers; no neutral module may import them.
Likewise, the general opening of `CommonAxisImageGeometry.lean` directly reexports
the neutral `DenseImage` declarations and contains no duplicate general proofs.

Final promotion to `Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension`
must follow only the neutral DAG above. The promoted module and every promoted
dependency must have no import of, reference to, or transitive dependency on the
LiNGAM paper namespace, research path, compatibility wrappers, or `Helpers/` tree.

## Target module (optional)
`Causalean.Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PolynomialImageDimension`

## Your tools
- Read the staging files and inspect the Causalean tree (root = the directory containing `Causalean.lean`; sources under `Causalean/`).
- Project search — run from `CausalSmith/tools`: `npm run search -- "<concepts>"` (find existing decls to reuse/dedup against), `npm run search -- --scope module "<concepts>"` (find the correct topical HOME file/area — use this for placement), `npm run search -- --type "<pattern>"` / `--goal "<goal>"`.
- lean-lsp for signatures / declaration files.

## What to decide
1. PLACEMENT — merge-first. Find the substrate's natural topical home with `--scope module`. STRONGLY prefer MERGING each decl into an existing `Causalean/.../X.lean`; only CREATE a new file for a genuinely new topic. `Causalean/Substrate/<Pascal>/` is a LAST RESORT, only when nothing fits — do not default to it (it becomes a junk drawer across many runs). Keep files under ~300 lines: if a merge would overflow, split into a sibling `Helpers/` file or a new topical file instead.
2. NAMESPACE — rewrite every `CausalSmith.Substrate.AffinePolynomialImageDimension` reference to the chosen Causalean namespace (the namespace of the file it lands in).
3. REUSE / DEDUP — for each decl, search Causalean for one that already proves the SAME statement. If it exists, drop your reinvented version and use the existing one (add its import, point references at it). Reuse ONLY on genuine statement-equality — a rewire to a decl that merely looks similar will break the build. Dedup edits stay inside YOUR new content; never delete or alter an existing Causalean decl.
4. DOCUMENTATION (docstring-canonical) — every public declaration gets a `/-- … -/` docstring whose FIRST paragraph is a self-contained natural-language translation for an econometrician with no Lean background (no Lean syntax, no field projections like `B.sigma` — say "the σ-algebra on B"; no "For every …" preamble restating the signature). Every new file opens with a `/-! … -/` module docstring stating what it provides.
5. HEADLINE curation — if a decl is a MAIN result (identification theorem, estimand characterization, named decomposition, asymptotic linearity/normality/rate/optimality/efficiency result, sharp bound), add an entry to `headline_theorems` in the matching `doc/library_review/<Area>.json`. Technical steps (measurability, rewrites, bridges, intermediate inequalities) stay supporting-tier.
6. API.md — for a NEW module, add a `<!-- GEN:Causalean.<Module> -->` / `<!-- /GEN -->` marker pair in `doc/API.md` where the per-decl table belongs (the system's `doc:gen` fills it — do NOT hand-write the table), plus any cross-cutting narrative for the new file.

## Output — WRITE bodies to staging, emit a TINY manifest
CRITICAL, read twice: do NOT put file contents inside the JSON. Large Lean bodies inside a JSON string are the #1 cause of a parse failure that aborts the whole integration (a truncated/mis-escaped string cannot be recovered). Instead:

1. WRITE every file body and every insert patch as a SEPARATE FILE under the staging directory:
   /<repo-root>/CausalSmith/doc/study/_substrate/affine-polynomial-image-dimension/coordinate_staging/round_2
   Use short names, e.g. `/<repo-root>/CausalSmith/doc/study/_substrate/affine-polynomial-image-dimension/coordinate_staging/round_2/foo.lean`, `/<repo-root>/CausalSmith/doc/study/_substrate/affine-polynomial-image-dimension/coordinate_staging/round_2/merge_x.lean`, `/<repo-root>/CausalSmith/doc/study/_substrate/affine-polynomial-image-dimension/coordinate_staging/round_2/area.json`. Put the EXACT bytes you want written/inserted into each staged file (namespaced, docstringed, ready to apply).
2. Emit ONLY a small JSON manifest that REFERENCES those staged files by `from` (relative to the staging dir). It contains no code — just short paths and one short anchor line per merge, so it always parses.

`{ "ops": [ … ], "notes": "<brief rationale>" }`. `target` paths are RELATIVE to the Causalean root. Op kinds:

- `{ "kind": "create_file", "target": "Causalean/…/Foo.lean", "from": "foo.lean", "newModule": "Causalean.….Foo" }` — write the staged `from` file as a NEW file at `target`. Set `newModule` (the Lean module name) ONLY for a new Lean module, so it is root-wired into `Causalean.lean`. Omit `newModule` for a new non-Lean record file.
- `{ "kind": "merge_lean", "target": "Causalean/…/X.lean", "anchor": "<a line already in X.lean>", "from": "merge_x.lean" }` — INSERT the staged `from` content after the first line containing `anchor` (empty anchor → append at end of file). INSERT-ONLY: the system preserves every existing byte and ROLLS BACK if a merge would change existing content. To add an import to an existing file, use a separate `merge_lean` anchored on an existing `import …` line (its `from` file holds just the import line). Multiple `merge_lean` ops may target the same file. If dependent inserts share one file/anchor, combine them into one staged file in dependency order, or anchor each later insert on text introduced by the prior one. Pick a SHORT, UNIQUE anchor line (a section header, a decl name) so it is unambiguous and easy to escape in JSON.
- `{ "kind": "write_file", "target": "doc/library_review/<Area>.json", "from": "area.json" }` — full-content write of a NON-Lean record file (headline sidecar, `doc/API.md`). Read the current file first, write the COMPLETE updated content to the staged `from` file (add entries/markers, never drop existing ones), and reference it here.

## Hard rules
- NEVER modify or delete an existing line in an existing Lean file. Integrating must be additive (new files + insert-only merges). If it genuinely requires editing an existing proven decl, do NOT — pick a different placement and explain in `notes`.
- Every `target` stays within the Causalean root; every `from` is a file you wrote under the staging dir.
- Do NOT run `lake build` / `library_index` / `embed` / `doc:gen` yourself — the system runs the full gate and rolls back on any failure. Emit only the manifest.
- Keep the manifest JSON small and valid: no code inside it, no trailing commas, all strings closed. If unsure, fewer ops with cleaner anchors.
